Tennessee House District 34

Representative Tim Rudd

Serving Rutherford County with a record built on property rights, election integrity, small business, and straightforward constituent service — from Murfreesboro to Smyrna.

Constituent Services Legislative Record
Office
Tennessee House of Representatives

District 34, Rutherford County
Current role
Chair, Elections & Campaign Finance Subcommittee

Biography

Rutherford County, through and through

Tim Rudd grew up in Murfreesboro and Smyrna, graduated from Smyrna High School, and attended Middle Tennessee State University. Before running for office he spent two decades as a real estate agent, direct mail consultant, and small business owner — the kind of career that builds a working knowledge of budgets, regulation, and what it actually takes to keep a business going.

His path into public life wasn't planned. A local annexation fight threatened a neighbor's property, and Rudd stepped up. That experience became the foundation of his approach to office: protect property rights, keep government accountable, and make sure ordinary people aren't steamrolled by bureaucracy.

His service is personal as well as professional. After his father died in 1984, Rudd helped support his mother through lean years. Later, he served as her primary caregiver as she battled dementia until her death at 91. Those years still shape his work on senior issues and family caregiving policy.

Steady, practical, and proud of Rutherford County — its growth and its character.
District 34Part of Rutherford County
Since 2017In the Tennessee House
ChairElections & Campaign Finance Subcommittee

Priorities

Focused on what matters at home

Rudd's work in Nashville is shaped by what he hears from families, business owners, seniors, and taxpayers across District 34.

Election integrity

As chair of the Elections and Campaign Finance Subcommittee, Rudd has worked to keep Tennessee's elections secure, transparent, and trustworthy.

Property rights

From his first fight in local government, Rudd has treated private property as a core constitutional issue — not an abstraction — and a practical concern for every homeowner and small business.

Small business

Two decades in the private sector gave Rudd a direct feel for what gets in the way of entrepreneurs. Tennessee grows when businesses can work, hire, and build without unnecessary red tape.

Senior issues

Years of caring for his mother through dementia gave Rudd a firsthand understanding of what families face. He carries that into his work on senior services and caregiver support.

Local government

Local decisions should be open and fair to taxpayers. Rudd pushes for government that answers to citizens — not insiders or special interests.

Public safety

Safe neighborhoods are the bedrock of a strong community. Rudd supports law enforcement, first responders, and policies that hold offenders accountable.

Public Record

An experienced voice in Nashville

Since taking office in 2017, Rudd has built a record around election integrity, lawful government, property rights, public safety, senior issues, and the conservative principles that keep Tennessee strong.

Apr 13, 2026

Advance notice for relocating homeless individuals

Rudd advanced legislation requiring notice before homeless individuals are relocated — bringing transparency and planning to a process that had too often operated without either.

Mar 19, 2026

Legislative process integrity and accountability

Rudd proposed reforms to strengthen how Tennessee's legislative process operates, making it harder for procedural games to undermine public trust.

Mar 17, 2026

Verification of lawful status in government hiring

Rudd passed legislation requiring verification of lawful status in state and local government hiring, keeping taxpayer-funded employment on the right side of the law.

Read more updates Official House profile

Endorsements

Trusted by Tennessee's leading organizations

Tim Rudd has earned the endorsement of organizations that fight for Second Amendment rights, pro-life values, and Tennessee small businesses.

Constituent Service

Helping District 34 navigate state government

Rudd's office is available to help with state agency questions, legislative concerns, and issues affecting Rutherford County families.

Whether you're a longtime Smyrna family, a Murfreesboro small business owner, a senior caregiver, or someone new to District 34 — the goal is the same: listen, respond, and help where state government is involved.

How the office can help

State agency concerns

Questions about state departments, licensing, benefits, records, or getting a timely response from an agency.

Legislative issues

Ideas, concerns, or feedback about bills, state policy, and priorities for the General Assembly.

Local impact

Issues affecting Rutherford County families, neighborhoods, small businesses, seniors, schools, and taxpayers.

Stay connected with Team Rudd

From neighborhood conversations to community events, the campaign is built on the same neighbor-to-neighbor trust that has defined Tim's service in Rutherford County.
